title: Operation params — reusable bundle of parameter values for a derivation
description:
One JSON per parameter bundle, under <registry_root>/operation_params/<operation_params_id>.json. Decoupled from derivation_v1 so two derivations can reuse the same bundle. The params object's shape depends on operation_type — same parameter bundle should only ever be referenced by derivations of the same operation_type. Per SETS_AND_ANALYSES.md §'Operation parameters'.

For thinning-style ops the bundle records distance_bp, sort_key, choice_rule, reset_scope, deterministic+seed. For window-extract ops it records window_size, step, chrom_filter. For random_sample it records n, seed, with_replacement. The 'extras' field is a free-form object for op-specific knobs that don't fit the canonical fields.

operation_type.description: Which operation kind this bundle is for (mirrors derivation_v1.operation_type). The 'params' shape is operation-type-specific.

params.description: Parameter values. Schema is operation-type-specific. Canonical fields per common operations are documented in _common_param_fields below; atlases may extend.

deterministic.description: True = bit-identical output for the same parent + params (no randomness). False = output depends on RNG state (seed should be set).

seed.description: RNG seed when deterministic=false. Null/absent when deterministic=true.

version_of_definition.description: Optional semver of the params interpretation. Lets you bump when you discover that a previously-accepted ambiguity in the params actually matters.

_common_param_fields._description: Documentation only — not validated. Canonical params keys per common operation_type.
thin_by_distance: distance_bp           (int)    minimum bp between kept sites
window_extract: step_bp               (int)    if sliding; absent for tiling

_examples._description: thin500 — deterministic, first valid site per 500 bp, reset per chromosome.
_examples.label: Thin to 1 site / 500 bp, first valid, per chromosome

_examples._description: Same distance, different choice rule, with seed.
_examples.label: Thin to 1 site / 500 bp, random per bin, seed 123
_examples.notes: DIFFERENT operation_params_id than thin500_first_per_chrom_v1 even though distance_bp is the same — the choice_rule and seed differ. Two sites_thin500_*_v1 sets that reference these will be DIFFERENT files.
